title: Tontelea laxiflora
taxonomy: Tontelea laxiflora is classified within the family Annonaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This classification places it within the order Magnoliales and the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Tontelea is a part of the family Annonaceae and includes various species of flowering plants. Tontelea laxiflora is a species within this genus, sharing characteristics and genetic traits with other members of the Annonaceae family. By classifying Tontelea laxiflora within the Annonaceae family, researchers can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Tontelea laxiflora, also known as Tontelea, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. Bioactive constituents of Tontelea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tontelea is commonly prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is also used in herbal teas and capsules to harness its potential health benefits.
